'''Craig Smith''' (born 26 February 1973) is an Australian former professional [[rugby league]] footballer who played in the 1990s.<ref name="RLP book">{{cite book| last1=Whiticker|first1=Alan|last2=Hudson|first2=Glen|title=The Encyclopedia of Rugby League Players|year=2007|publisher=Gary Allen Pty Ltd|location=[[Wetherill Park, New South Wales]]|isbn=978-1-877082-93-1|pages=609}}</ref>

==Biography==
Smith made his first grade rugby league debut for [[North Sydney Bears]] in 1995, playing two games late in the season, including the Bears' qualifying final defeat.<ref>{{cite web |title=ARL 1995 – Qualif Final |url=https://www.rugbyleagueproject.org/seasons/arl-1995/qualifying-final/newcastle-vs-north-sydney/summary.html |publisher=Rugby League Project |access-date=21 July 2021}}</ref>
Joining [[Canterbury-Bankstown Bulldogs]] during [[Super League (Australia)|Super League]], Smith scored 20 points in the Bulldogs' 1997 reserve grade premiership win over [[Auckland Warriors]], before signing a two-year contract to play with [[Melbourne Storm]], who entered the [[National Rugby League|NRL]] in [[1998 NRL season|1998]].<ref>{{cite web |title=Storm players (1998) |url=http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/players/pla_2.htm |website=melbournestorm.com.au |access-date=21 July 2021 |archive-url=https://web.archive.org/web/19990128034738fw_/http://www.melbournestorm.com.au/players/pla_2.htm |archive-date=28 January 1999}}</ref>

Smith was [[Melbourne Storm]]'s leading point-scorer in the club's [[1998 Melbourne Storm season|debut year]] (110 points), but had to sit on the sidelines for much of 1999 due to the good form of [[Matt Geyer]]. Smith trained in Melbourne, but flew back to Brisbane on weekends to play with the Storm's feeder club, [[Norths Devils]], in the [[Queensland Cup]] competition.

Smith was re-instated on the wing before the 1999 semi-final series when coach [[Chris Anderson (rugby league)|Chris Anderson]] dropped his son Ben and moved Geyer to {{rlp|fe}}.  Anderson also made Smith the primary goal-kicker. Smith's boot proved the difference in three sudden-death victories, culminating in his three goals and penalty try from the wing in the [[1999 NRL Grand Final]], despite playing with knee injuries and a broken rotator cuff in his shoulder.<ref name="DT2018">{{cite news |last1=Walshaw |first1=Nick |title=Storm rescue service |work=[[The Daily Telegraph (Sydney)|The Daily Telegraph]]|location=Sydney |date=29 September 2018 |page=100}}</ref>

Smith rejected Melbourne's offer for the [[2000 NRL season]] and retired.<ref name="RLP book" /> After moving to Perth where he lived for many years,<ref name="DT2018"/> he now lives back in Melbourne.

In 2017, Smith spoke about his time in rugby league saying "We were walking around in Melbourne and were three times the size of everyone else, that is how they knew who the rugby league players were back then, nobody knew anything about the sport. At our first game they were cheering and I don’t think they knew what they were cheering about".<ref>{{Cite web|url=https://www.melbournestorm.com.au/news/2017/05/18/where-are-they-now-craig-smith/|title=Where are they now: Craig Smith|date=18 May 2017}}</ref>
